name: Galápagos Marine Iguana
conservationEfforts: Galápagos National Park management. Invasive species control. Marine protection around the islands. Population crashes during severe El Niño events.
description: The world's only ocean-going lizard, diving up to 20 meters to graze on algae. They sneeze out concentrated salt through special glands. Each island has a distinct subspecies with different coloring.
